question_text: Which of the following statements correctly describes the difference between speed and velocity?

options.label: A
options.text: Speed is how fast something moves; velocity is how fast it moves in a particular direction

options.label: B
options.text: Velocity is always faster than speed

options.label: C
options.text: Speed and velocity mean exactly the same thing

options.label: D
options.text: Velocity only applies to objects moving in a straight line

explanation: Speed is a scalar quantity (just the magnitude), while velocity is a vector (magnitude and direction). This is why a car going 30 m/s north has a different velocity than one going 30 m/s south, even though both have the same speed.
